rulings.Case Title: JOHNA PECOT et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION, A et al

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOE DRUMMOND et al VS. FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA MUTUAL BENEFIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas Purpostedly Served On Canyon Pacific Management Co., Inc. And Eugene Burger Management Corporation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 9, 2014, Line 1, RESPONDENT FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA MUTUAL BENEFIT Motion To Quash Subpoenas Purportedly Served On Canyon Pacific Management Co., Inc. And Eugene Burger Management Corporation. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties to report on agreements reached at the "meet and confer" process undertaken since the previous hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KEN LAM et al VS. CALIFORNIA CAPITAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 9, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA CAPITAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled. First, the documents submitted with Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice fail to disprove that California Insurance Group ("CIG") is a legal entity, and there are sufficient allegations in the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") to establish a basis for CIG's liability. Second, Defendants inherently acknowledge that, because the insurance policy was not attached to the FAC, it is not apparent from the face of the Complaint that CIG was not a party to the contract. Thus, Defendants' failure to request judicial notice of the policy forecloses a demurrer on this grounds as a demurrer can be used only to challenge defects appearing on the face of the pleading or from matters that are properly judicially noticed. Next, the FAC states a valid cause of action for negligence by sufficiently alleging the wrongful conduct of the broker and that the broker was acting as an agent of Defendants. (See Desai v. Farmers Ins. Exchange (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 1110 [holding that if an agent fails to exercise reasonable care in procuring the type of insurance that the insured demanded and bargained for, the insurer may be liable].) Additionally, the FAC states valid causes of action for fraud and negligent misrepresentation (see FAC Paragraphs 5, 68, 76, 78-79), and the allegations are not uncertain. Finally, Defendants' attempt to specially demur to the third and fourth causes of action are overruled. (See CRC 3.1113(a) [providing for waiver of all grounds of special demurrers not supported in the memorandum of points and authorities].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA L. TANACEA VS. RICHARD R. TAVERNETTI MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 9, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT EXACTECH, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The Third Amended Complaint does not allege with the requisite particularity that Dr. Tavernetti was acting as Exactech's agent at the time the misrepresentations/concealments were made. The general allegations of agency alleged in paragraph 2 are insufficient, as fraud must be pled with particularity. The Third Amended Complaint also does not state facts establishing that Exactech ratified or authorized Dr. Tavernetti's statements. No facts are pled establishing that Exactech knew about the statements that Dr. Tavernetti made to Plaintiff. Although Plaintiff has been given multiple opportunities to amend, leave to amend is granted because there is a "reasonable possibility that the defect can be cured by amendment." (Goodman v. Kennedy (1976) 18 Cal.3d 335, 349.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ERLINDA GABUYO VS. PARK MERCED INVESTMENT PROPERTIES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Issue Sanctions And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions And For Monetary Sactions For Altering Or Destroying Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 9, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ERLINDA GABUYO Motion For Issue Sanctions And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions And For Monetary Sanctions For Altering Or Destroying Evidence. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties are directed to produce all original photographs in their possessions of the subject sidewalk location. Defendant shall produce all discoverable reports and notes of the inspection by its expert, Jay Mandell, Ph.D. The Court is inclined to deny the motion without prejudice. The sidewalk repair was performed in accordance with a scheduled capital improvement project and not to intentionally deprive plaintiff of an opportunity to inspect the location. Plaintiff's motion does not indicate prejudice sufficient to warrant imposition of issue or evidentiary sanctions. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required and parties to appear. The Court discloses his law firm recently filed a lawsuit against defendant Regents (and others) in Sacramento County in connection with representation of a client alleging medical negligence against health care providers. The Court does not believe the pendency of the Sacramento lawsuit prejudices or biases him as to any party before the Court or to effect his ability to decide the issues raised by this motion. The parties are invited to address this disclosure at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL LAURY ET AL VS. GEARY LSF GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Geary Lsf Group, Inc.'S Motion To Vacate Writ Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 9, 2014, Line 12, Defendant Geary LSF Group, Inc.'s Motion To Vacate Writ Order. Motion granted. Plaintiffs have not established the probable validity of the claim. The subordination agreements render Plaintiffs' notes payable only after Defendant's obligation to pay the senior debt has been discharged, and thus, Plaintiffs' repayment is not yet collectable. (See Soundlite Systems, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1976) 65 Cal.App.3d 78.) Further, whether the subordination agreements of any newly incurred debt apply to Plaintiffs' payment priority is irrelevant in determining the priority of Plaintiffs' notes until the senior debt to which Plaintiffs contractually subordinated their notes is satisfied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
